background: We wish to estimate the mean weight of a population in which body weight has a log-Normal distribution with $\sigma=40$ pounds. Three independent SRS each of $n=712$ are selected from the population.

task.query: Calculate $95 \%$ confidence intervals for $\mu$ based on the first sample with $\bar{x}_1=183$.
task.answer: For sample 1, the confidence interval is $\bar{x}_1 \pm m=183 \pm 3=180$ to 186 (pounds).

task.query: Calculate $95 \%$ confidence intervals for $\mu$ based on the second sample with $\bar{x}_2=180$.
task.answer: For sample 2, the confidence interval is $\bar{x}_2 \pm m=180 \pm 3=177$ to 183 (pounds).

task.query: Calculate $95 \%$ confidence intervals for $\mu$ based on the third sample with $\bar{x}_3=184$.
task.answer: For sample 3, the confidence interval is $\bar{x}_3 \pm m=184 \pm 3=181$ to 187 (pounds).

meta info.caption: 95 % confidence intervals for $\mu, \sigma$ known (Body weight, 20-29-year-old males).
meta info.section: 10.2 Confidence Intervals for $\\mu$ When $\\sigma$ Is Known
